title: Pesticide chemistry verification process: Updated work plan and outcomes - Canada.ca
content:
## Pesticide chemistry verification process: Updated work plan and outcomes
Health Canada 7 April 2026
- Active  ingredients subject to chemistry verification (by year, 2025 to 2030)

## Document  history (revision/update)
Update and rationale
PMRA confirmed active ingredients subject to  chemistry verification in 2026-2027.
Outcomes    for active ingredients subject to chemistry verification in 2024-2025 were added.
Health Canada’s Pest Management Regulatory Agency (PMRA)  initiated and confirmed active ingredients subject to chemistry verification in  2025-2026 and in other years. Iodosulfuron-methyl-sodium, prohexadione calcium,  and German cockroach extract are now subject to chemistry verification in  2026-2027 due to recent re-evaluation activity.
Initial    publication of Pesticide Chemistry Verification Process and 2024-2025 work    plan
Health  Canada has modernized its pesticide review process through the  implementation of a continuous oversight model . As part of this process modernization effort,  Health Canada’s approach for verifying  product chemistry information of technical grade active ingredient products is  updated.
Verifying product chemistry as part of continuous oversight is an  important component for ensuring that health and environmental risk assessments  are relying on the best available chemistry information. This initiative will  contribute to improved monitoring of pesticide active ingredients to confirm  they are being manufactured consistent with approved specifications and  methods. It will also ensure that  Health Canada’s chemistry review is  up-to-date when an active undergoes re-evaluation, resulting in more timely  decisions.
To provide registrants with advance notice of  when their active ingredients will be subject to chemistry verification and to  keep other interested stakeholders informed,  Health Canada is maintaining a  transparent 5-year chemistry verification work plan, which will be updated  annually. Footnote 1
Through this updated process, chemistry verification for each active  ingredient is expected to occur at least every 10 years, with the flexibility  to require chemistry verification sooner when warranted. This  includes considering actives as identified using the continuous oversight  model,  proportional effort policy, and  other scenarios. For example:
- When actives or sources are known to contain impurities or       contaminants of health or environmental concern
- When  Health       Canada becomes aware of a potential issue that requires       further assessment
- When there is a history of discrepancies in specifications for       certain actives or sources
Registrants will be requested to submit listed chemistry information  regarding their active ingredient, including quality control data, description  of the current manufacturing process and up-to-date product specification form. When needed,  Health  Canada will request additional GLP-compliant Footnote 2 chemistry batch data and/or impurity of concern data to support  continued registration.
If the chemistry verification determines that the product  composition is not within approved specifications, or the manufacturing process  has changed without proper reporting, Health Canada may consider appropriate  compliance and enforcement actions.
Additional details on the process are provided in the  “Memorandum to Registrants: Verifying Chemistry of Registered Technical Grade Active  Ingredients and Integrated System Products ”.
This webpage presents published work plans as well  as outcomes from previous years.

## Active ingredients subject to chemistry verification (by year, 2025 to 2030)

### 2025-2026 (confirmed)
Registrants  of active ingredients on the FY2025-26 list have received a request to provide  chemistry verification information for their registered technical products in  May 2025 with a submission deadline of 90 days. The review process is expected  to be completed at the end of March 2026.
- 1,2-Benzisothiazolin-3-one, BIT
- 6-Benzylaminopurine (or: 6-benzyladenine)
- Saponins of Chenopodium quinoa
- 2,4-D (present as amine salts: dimethylamine  salt, diethanolamine salt, or other amine salts)
- 2,4-D (present as low volatile esters)
- 2,4-D (present as choline salt)
- Dicamba (present as acid, ester, salt)
- Ethametsulfuron-methyl
- Gibberellins A4 and A7 (omitted from Year 1)
- Mandipropamid Footnote 3
- MCPA (present as acid)
- MCPA (present as amine salts: diethanolamine,  dimethylamine, or mixed amines)
- MCPA (present as esters)
- MCPA (present as potassium salt or as sodium  salt)
- Oxirane derivatives (50% minimum)
- 2-Phenylphenol (present as potassium salt)
- 2-Phenylphenol (present as sodium salt)
- Tetrakis (hydroxymethyl) phosphonium sulfate  (THPS)

### 2026-2027 (confirmed)
Active ingredients subject to chemistry verification in 2026-27 are confirmed. Registrants of these active ingredients will receive a request to provide chemistry verification information for their registered technical products in May 2026 with a submission deadline of 90 days. The review process is expected to be completed by the end of March 2027.
- n-Alkyl(25% c12, 60% c14, 15%  c16)dimethylbenzylammonium chloride
- Alkyl(40% c12, 50% c14, 10%  c16)dimethylbenzylammonium chloride
